summary: Go-based test orchestration framework with agent-driven validation, TUI interface, and MCP integration for running and reporting structured test suites
patterns: test-driven development with extensive extra test suites
command pattern via cmd/ directory structure
agent-based orchestration (GOrchestra with numbered agent directories)
reporter pattern for test output (webhook, push)
concerns: large number of test agent directories (0005-0039+) suggests complex orchestration overhead
truncated directory names indicate filesystem constraints or naming issues
multiple extra test files may indicate incomplete base test coverage
wave-based dispatch pattern could introduce synchronization complexity
go.mod stub suggests dependency management may be in flux
suggestions: consolidate agent test directories into a unified test matrix
document the GOrchestra orchestration workflow and agent lifecycle
review assertion coverage gaps that require extra test files
establish clear boundaries between internal packages (runner, schema, reporter, mcp)
consider integration test deduplication across agent directories
